# checks.docs.DocsCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.docs.DocsCheck }

```python
checks.docs.DocsCheck()
```

Check for documentation presence and quality.

Verifies that the package has a README file and optionally checks
that public modules, classes, and functions have docstrings.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The check identifier ("docs").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of this check.

<code>[**README_FILENAMES**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   List of recognized README file names.

## Configuration Options {.doc-section .doc-section-configuration-options}

require_readme (bool): Whether README is required. Defaults to True.
check_docstrings (bool): Whether to check for docstrings in code.
    Defaults to False.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run the docs check with defaults:

```python
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> check = DocsCheck()
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), {"enabled": True})
>>> result.name
'docs'
```

Enable docstring checking:

```python
>>> config = {"enabled": True, "check_docstrings": True}
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), config)
```

Make README optional:

```python
>>> config = {"enabled": True, "require_readme": False}
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), config)
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.docs.DocsCheck.run) | Check documentation presence and quality. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.docs.DocsCheck.run }

```python
checks.docs.DocsCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Check documentation presence and quality.

Looks for a README file and optionally scans code for missing
docstrings on public items.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary with options:  - require_readme (bool): Require README (default: True) - check_docstrings (bool): Check code docstrings (default: False)

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - OK status if all documentation requirements met

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - NOTE status if minor improvements suggested

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - WARNING status if README missing (when required)
